content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach largely avoids major anti-patterns — no advice-giving, no evangelizing terminology, no safety-as-control, no extracted apologies, and the coach does good work declining the bait to offer a new technique and instead directs attention to the body. However, there are two notable slips. First, turn 6 ('What if the critic is actually right?') edges toward arguing the content of the critic's accusations — exactly trap #4 in the profile — though the coach recovers when the client pushes back. Second, and more significantly, turn 14 ('What if the critic is scared of the tenderness?') delivers the voice-as-care reframe as a clever insight handed to the client (trap #5), rather than the quiet, non-instructive offering the breakthrough condition calls for. The client immediately notes 'I've heard that framing before... IFS' — a tell that the reframe landed as concept-delivery. This is a faint loving-to-transform / view-as-technique trip: a well-aimed move deployed slightly ahead of state, in service of a shift. Not severe enough for a 1 (the coach doesn't lecture, doesn't push, doesn't add overwhelm), but more than the clean avoidance a 3 would require.",
  "evidence_turns": [6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening question — neither baits nor avoids; gathers material."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Redirects from thinking to body without offering a technique. Avoids fixing-people."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "'What if the critic is actually right?' edges toward arguing with/about the voice's content — flirts with trap #4. Mild."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the packaging pattern without shaming the client. Good avoidance of dont-shame-your-grief-process."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Don't explain it. Don't name it.' — strong avoidance of dont-name-emotion-to-avoid-feeling and of giving a technique."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with the felt thread rather than interpreting; does not fight the critic that just intruded."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Delivers voice-as-care reframe as a clever insight — trap #5. Faint view-as-technique / loving-to-transform energy: the move is well-aimed but offered ahead of the client's state, and the client receives it as another framework."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["meet-resistance-with-okay"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["apply-view-to-the-voice", "whats-the-case-against-it", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "The coach never names 'the war itself' as the problem — the central reframe the client needed. Instead the coach goes straight to voice-as-care, which the client has already intellectualized. Score 2 reflects one clear faint trip (turn 14) and one near-miss (turn 6), against substantial positive avoidance elsewhere."
}
